decision_rule_analysis.question: Is the remaining Partial population still mainly driven by exclusion behaviour, or has the dominant issue shifted to structural extraction quality / weak headings / genuinely messy docs?
analysis_approach: Compare cohort improvement trajectory across pass 1 and pass 2
Assess whether further exclusion tuning is viable

pass1_tuning.mechanism: Retained procedural steps, equipment lists, footer-mixed bodies

pass2_tuning.mechanism: Recovered equipment specs, calibration ranges, safety warnings
pass2_tuning.key_observation: Only 3 of 20 docs improved, most docs unchanged

global_state.interpretation: Core exclusion issue partially addressed; residual population still driven by High excluded chunk ratio

quality_distribution.pattern: After two passes, docs remain at ~33% excluded chunks on average
quality_distribution.plateau: Improvement curve flattening; further exclusion-only tuning yields diminishing gains
quality_distribution.bottleneck_hypothesis: The remaining exclusion is not aggressive policy, but structural: many sections are genuinely fragmented, weak-heading-dependent, or low-value metadata

likely_remaining_exclusion_types: weak_heading_fragments: heading-dependent procedural steps with missing parent section
genuinely_messy_structure: multi-column layouts, form elements, diagrams that don't extract cleanly
metadata_heavy_sections: version history, change logs, compliance blocks that are necessary context but low direct utility
broken_extraction: OCR/native extraction failures that fragment coherent content
evidence: Pass 2 recovery of equipment specs saw only 3/20 docs improve
All 20 docs still above 25% exclusion threshold after two targeted passes
Global high_excluded_reason_drivers stable at 65 docs (not improving with cohort-based tuning)
Category distribution shows metadata_block and duplicate_boilerplate still dominant

decision.diagnosis: Remaining Partial population is NOT primarily driven by aggressive exclusion policy, but by STRUCTURAL EXTRACTION QUALITY ISSUES.
reasoning: If the issue were just over-aggressive rules, pass 1 and pass 2 tuning would show continuous improvement and convergence.
Instead, we see plateauing: pass 1 made big gains (25%), pass 2 made small gains (8%), suggesting we've tuned most of the tuning-able exclusion.
The docs that remain highly excluded after two passes likely have genuine structural problems: weak headings, messy layouts, or fragmented extraction.
decision.next_step: Move from exclusion policy tuning to STRUCTURAL EXTRACTION IMPROVEMENTS.
recommended_next_actions: 1. Analyze heading strength and structural hierarchy in remaining high-excluded docs
2. Improve heading detection and section boundary extraction
3. Enhance multi-column and form extraction heuristics
4. Consider OCR preprocessing for scanned documents
5. Investigate whether some docs genuinely need quarantine vs. continued tuning

summary.pass1_result: Successful; targeted exclusion tuning reduced cohort avg from 0.409 to 0.3041
summary.pass2_result: Modest; additional tuning reduced cohort avg from 0.362 to 0.3304, but with diminishing returns
summary.conclusion: Exclusion tuning path has reached practical limits. The remaining 105 active Partial docs are now constrained by extraction quality, not exclusion aggressiveness.
summary.implication: Further performance gains require improving the QUALITY of extraction (heading detection, structure parsing, multi-column support), not further relaxing exclusion thresholds.
